Eclipse JDT official documentation (https://help.eclipse.org/latest/topic/org.eclipse.jdt.doc.user/reference/preferences/java/ref-preferences-compiler.htm) confirms this preference key controls 'Reporting Raw Type Reference' with valid values: error, warning, info, ignore
Eclipse JDT official documentation confirms this preference key controls 'Reporting Unused Import' with valid values: error, warning, info, ignore

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic test data: six Java files (DataProcessor.java, CacheManager.java, StringHelper.java, SortedBuffer.java, Pair.java, Registry.java) created programmatically in /home/ga/project-sources/DataUtils with intentional compiler warnings including raw types, unused imports, missing serialVersionUID, resource leaks, and deprecated API usage. The project will be imported to Eclipse workspace at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/DataUtils. Task requires configuring Eclipse Java Compiler preferences for org.eclipse.jdt.core.compiler.problem.rawTypeReference and org.eclipse.jdt.core.compiler.problem.unusedImport to 'error'. Success requires clean compilation with zero warnings and errors, verified by javac -Xlint:all returning no output.
